domains.create: Click here to create a new <bold>Domain</bold>.
domains.introDescription: <bold>Domains</bold> are collections of related data assets associated with a specific part of your organization, such as the <bold>Marketing</bold> department.
entityProfile.documentationTabDescriptio: You can view and edit an entity's <bold>Documentation</bold> on this tab.
<bold>Documentation</bold> should provide descriptive information about this data asset. It can also contain links to external resources.
entityProfile.domainTabDescription1: You can view and set this asset's <bold>Domain</bold> here.
entityProfile.entitiesTabDescription: You can view the <bold>Entities</bold> that belong to a <bold>Container</bold> on this tab.
entityProfile.glossaryTermsTabDescriptio: You can view and add <bold>Glossary Terms</bold> to this asset here.

<bold>Glossary Terms</bold> are structured, standardized labels for organizing your mission-critical data. For example, if you're marking assets containing PII fields, you might add the Term <bold>Email</bold>.
entityProfile.lineageTabDescription1: You can view an entity's <bold>Lineage</bold> on this tab.
entityProfile.lineageTabDescription3: If this tab is disabled, <bold>Lineage</bold> has not been ingested for this entity.
entityProfile.ownersTabDescription1: You can view and add <bold>Owners</bold> to this asset here.
entityProfile.ownersTabDescription2: <bold>Owners</bold> are <bold>Users</bold> or <bold>Groups</bold> who are responsible for managing this asset.
entityProfile.propertiesTabDescription1: You can view an entity's key-value <bold>Properties</bold> on this tab. These are sourced from the original Data Platform.
entityProfile.propertiesTabDescription2: If this tab is disabled, <bold>Properties</bold> have not been ingested for this entity.
entityProfile.schemaTabDescription1: You can view a Dataset's <bold>Schema</bold> on this tab.
entityProfile.schemaTabDescription2: You can also view or add <bold>Documentation</bold>, <bold>Tags</bold>, and <bold>Glossary Terms</bold> for specific columns.
entityProfile.sidebarDescription: The asset sidebar is a vertically organized set of important information about an asset. It shows up on the right side of the screen when you view an asset, a search result, a lineage entry and a host of other places.
entityProfile.tagsTabDescription1: You can view and add <bold>Tags</bold> to this asset here.
entityProfile.tagsTabDescription2: <bold>Tags</bold> are labels for organizing your data. For example, you can add a Tag to mark an asset as <bold>Mission Critical</bold>.
entityProfileV2.columnsDescription1: You can view an asset's <bold>Fields</bold> on this tab.
entityProfileV2.columnsDescription2: You can also view or add <bold>Documentation</bold>, <bold>Tags</bold>, and <bold>Glossary Terms</bold> for specific fields.
entityProfileV2.columnsDescription3: Click on individual fields to view more details such as statistics and properties.
entityProfileV2.columnsTabDescription: Quick access to an asset's <bold>Fields</bold> on this tab.
entityProfileV2.contentsDescription: You can view the child <bold>Assets</bold> that belong to this <bold>Asset</bold> on this tab.
entityProfileV2.documentationDescription: You can view and edit rich <bold>Documentation</bold> on this tab.
<bold>Documentation</bold> should provide descriptive information about this data asset to help your data explorers understand it better. It can also contain links to external resources.
entityProfileV2.incidentsDescription1: View and manage <bold>Incidents</bold> on this tab.
entityProfileV2.incidentsDescription2: Incidents are issues or events that require attention. They can be related to data quality, governance, schema changes, and more.
entityProfileV2.lineageDescription: Quick access to upstream and downstream lineage, both direct and indirect.
entityProfileV2.propertiesDescription1: Properties have moved to the asset sidebar! You can view and (soon) edit <bold>Properties</bold> on this tab.
entityProfileV2.propertiesDescription2: Properties are key value pairs that provide additional information about an asset. Some properties are ingested from the source data platform, while others are added by users.
entityProfileV2.qualityDescription2: Quality information includes <bold>data contracts</bold> and data quality test results.
entityProfileV2.queriesDescription1: View highlighted and relevant <bold>Queries</bold> on this tab.
entityProfileV2.queriesDescription2: Highlighted queries are handpicked by your data experts to help you get started with this asset.
entityProfileV2.queriesDescription3: Relevant queries are computed based on recency, popularity, and other factors.
entityProfileV2.sidebarDescription: The asset sidebar is a vertically organized set of important information about an asset. It appears on the right side of the screen when viewing an asset, a search result, a lineage entry, and many other places.
entityProfileV2.summaryDescription: Quick access to at-a-glance information about the asset.
glossary.createTerm: Click here to create a new <bold>Term</bold>.
glossary.createTermGroup: Click here to create a new <bold>Term Group</bold>.
glossary.introDescription: The Glossary is a collection of structured, standardized labels you can use to categorize data assets. You can view and create both <bold>Terms</bold> and <bold>Term Groups</bold> here.
glossary.termGroupsDescription: <bold>Term Groups</bold> act as folders, containing Terms and nested Term Groups.
glossary.termGroupsExample: For example, there could be a <bold>PII Term Group</bold> containing Terms for different types of PII, such as <bold>Email</bold> or <bold>Phone Number</bold>.
glossary.termsDescription: <bold>Terms</bold> are words or phrases with a specific business definition assigned to them.
groups.create: Click here to create a new <bold>Group</bold>.
groups.introDescription1: <bold>Groups</bold> are collections of users which can be used to assign ownership to assets and manage access.
groups.introDescription2: <bold>Groups</bold> can be created natively within DataHub, or synced from your Identity Provider.
home.announcementsDescription: The <bold>Announcements</bold> tab contains important updates and information from your organization. Be sure to check it out frequently!
home.bulletGovernance: Create and manage <bold>central governance standards</bold> for data assets to drive accountability and trust
home.bulletInsights: Gain <bold>insights</bold> about how others within your organization are using data
home.bulletLineage: View and understand the full <bold>end-to-end Lineage</bold> of how data is created, transformed, and consumed
home.bulletLineageV2: View and understand the full <bold>end-to-end lineage</bold> of how data is created, transformed, and consumed
home.bulletOwnership: Define <bold>ownership</bold> and capture <bold>knowledge</bold> to empower others
home.bulletQuality: Understand <bold>quality</bold> and trustworthiness of data using operational and social signals
home.bulletSearch: Quickly <bold>search</bold> for Datasets, Dashboards, Data Pipelines, and more
home.bulletSearchV2: Quickly <bold>search</bold> for Tables, Dashboards, Data Pipelines, and more
home.discoverDescription: The <bold>Discover</bold> section serves as your exploration center for discovering new areas of your data estate. You can explore Domains, Platforms, and more.
home.domainsDescription: Here are your organization's <bold>Domains</bold>. Domains are collections of data assets - such as Tables, Dashboards, and ML Models - that make it easy to discover information relevant to a particular part of your organization.
home.exploreAll: Not sure where to start? Click on <bold>Explore All</bold>!
home.ingestionDescription: Start integrating your data sources immediately by navigating to the <bold>Ingestion</bold> page.
home.mostPopularDescription: Here you'll find the assets that are viewed most frequently within your organization.
home.personalSidebarDescription: This is your <bold>Personal Sidebar</bold>. It contains links to assets you own, groups you are in, your subscriptions and more.
home.platformsDescription: Here are your organization's <bold>Data Platforms</bold>. Data Platforms represent specific third-party Data Systems or Tools. Examples include Data Warehouses like <bold>Snowflake</bold>, Orchestrators like <bold>Airflow</bold>, and Dashboarding tools like <bold>Looker</bold>.
home.searchBarDescription: This is the <bold>Search Bar</bold>. It will serve as your launch point for discovering and collaborating around the data most important to you.
home.tutorialHotkey: Press <bold>Cmd + Ctrl + T</bold> to open up this tutorial at any time.
home.viewsDescription: Views help you focus on the assets that you care about. You can switch between views using the dropdown. Your admin will configure the views that make sense for your organization. You can customize and create your own views as well.
home.welcomeIntro: <bold>DataHub</bold> helps you discover and organize the important data within your organization. You can:
home.welcomeIntroV2: <bold>DataHub</bold> helps you discover, govern and ensure high quality for the important data within your organization. You can:
ingestion.createSourceDescription: Configure new Integrations from DataHub to your <bold>Data Platforms</bold>, including Transactional Databases like <bold>MySQL</bold>, Data Warehouses such as <bold>Snowflake</bold>, Dashboarding tools like <bold>Looker</bold>, and more!
ingestion.learnMore: Learn more about ingestion and view the full list of supported Integrations <anchor>here.</anchor>
ingestion.refreshDescription: Click to force a refresh of running ingestion sources.
lineageGraph.dataLineageDescription: Data <bold>Lineage</bold> allows you to visualize and understand both the upstream dependencies and downstream consumers of this entity.
lineageGraph.introDescription: You can view the <bold>Lineage Graph</bold> for an entity on this page.
lineageGraph.timeFilterDescription: You can click which dates you would like to see lineage edges for on this graph. By default, the graph will show edges observed in the last 14 days. Note that manual lineage edges and edges without time information will always be shown.
policies.create: Click here to create a new <bold>Policy</bold>.
policies.introDescription1: In most cases, <bold>Roles</bold> are the best option for granting privileges to DataHub users.
policies.introDescription2: When more fine-grained control over user and group permissions is required, then <bold>Policies</bold> will do the trick.
roles.introDescription1: <bold>Roles</bold> are the recommended way to manage permissions on DataHub.
roles.introDescription2: DataHub currently supports three out-of-the-box Roles: <bold>Admin</bold>, <bold>Editor</bold> and <bold>Reader</bold>.
roles.learnMore: Learn more about <bold>Roles</bold> and the different permissions for each Role <anchor>here</anchor>.
search.advancedDescription: <bold>Advanced Filters</bold> offer additional capabilities to create more specific search queries.
search.browseDescription: Have a clear idea of the schema or folder you're searching for? Easily navigate your organization's platforms inline. Then select a specific container you want to filter your results by.
search.browseTitle: 🧭 Explore and refine your search by platform
search.filtersDescription: Quickly find relevant assets by applying one or more filters. Try filtering by <bold>Type</bold>, <bold>Owner</bold>, and more!
search.filtersMovedDescription: Quickly find relevant assets with our new and improved filter interface! Our latest update has relocated filters to the top of the screen for ease of access.
users.assignRoleDescription: You can assign <bold>Roles</bold> to existing <bold>Users</bold> here.
users.introDescription: There are a few different ways to onboard new <bold>Users</bold> onto DataHub.
users.inviteDescription: Easily share an invite link with your colleagues to onboard them onto DataHub. Optionally assign a <bold>Role</bold> to anyone who joins using the link.
users.inviteLearnMore: Learn more about configuring invite links <anchor>here.</anchor>
users.ssoDescription: The preferred way to onboard new <bold>Users</bold> is to use <bold>Single Sign-On</bold>. Currently, DataHub supports OIDC SSO.
users.ssoLearnMore: Learn more about how to configure <bold>Single Sign-On</bold> <anchor>here.</anchor>
welcome.slideDocsDescription: Save hours of manual work while improving discoverability
welcome.slideFindDescription: Search datasets, models, dashboards, and more across your entire stack
welcome.slideImpactDescription: Preview the full impact of schema and column changes
welcome.slideLineageDescription: See the full story of how your data was created and transformed
welcome.slideReadyDescription: Explore our comprehensive documentation or jump right in and start discovering your data
